title: INtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Uzbekistan’s Uzum valuation leaps over 50% in seven months to $2.3B
items.description: Uzum now reaches 20 million users and processes $11B in payments as it expands its e-commerce and fintech ecosystem across Uzbekistan.

items.title: Whoop launches a new blood test focused on women’s health

items.title: 8 Best Cheap Phones (2026), Tested and Reviewed
items.description: There’s little reason to pay top dollar for a smartphone. These iPhones and Android devices—ranging from $100 to $600—stood up to WIRED’s testing.

items.title: How to Responsibly Dispose of Your Electronics (2026)
items.description: Get rid of old, broken, and unused devices—even Lightning cables—without adding to the e-waste problem.

items.title: Apple now makes one in four iPhones in India: report
items.description: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

items.title: How Can a Locomotive Pull a Long Train That’s Much Heavier?
items.description: For railroads, it’s all about managing static and kinetic friction.

items.description: Can you really get a Windows PC running like new again? Using this free tool from Microsoft, you can get pretty close.
